summary: Multi-agent research program: find untried attack routes on H(668)/C(334)/srg(333)

result.report:
# H(668) / srg(333,166,82,83) — Synthesis Report and Research Program

Synthesis of: 8 symmetry-atlas ansätze, 8 literature hunts, 10 idea-tournament proposals, and adversarial verification (refutation + prior-art) on the promising subset. All claims below carry the verification verdicts; refuted items are marked as such and their salvage stated.

---

## 1. TOP 3 ACTIONS (ranked by P(new result) × 1/effort)

### ACTION 1 — Self-complementary Z37⋊Z4 anti-automorphism search (idea I10; promise HIGH; survived two adversarial verifications)

The only route in the entire portfolio that is simultaneously: new to the literature, new to the project, gated down to a tiny instance count, and double-checked. The complement-symmetry filter on the project's **certified-complete** orbit-matrix enumeration leaves exactly **3 of 625 classes** (indices 109, 221, 422 in `orbit_all_classes.txt`; independently reproduced twice; 2/2/4 complementing permutations each, all cycle type (4,4,1), fixed-orbit diagonal 18; the 26 surviving representatives in `orbit_all_sym.txt` collapse to the same 3 classes — gate K1 is already discharged).

- **Exact first step:** for each of the 3 classes × each complementing permutation, solve the linear twist-consistency system over Z37 in b_1..b_8 (one Z37 condition per size-4 block-orbit, plus γ = 6β₁+β₂ = 0 at each antipodal orbit; b_9 gauge). Then emit CP-SAT instances with the **verifier-corrected encoding: 415 binary variables, NOT 379 — the two antipodal blocks are FULLY FREE (37 bits each); the original "central symmetry forced" claim was refuted by explicit counterexample (Z13 model, 117 vertices). Imposing it makes UNSAT meaningless.** Mandatory regression gate: validate the encoding on the Z13/multiplier-5 true-positive model and Paley anchors before any 333-run.
- **Tools/compute:** existing `cyclo_cpsat.py`/Modal pipeline; ~1 week dev; local first, <$500 Modal if needed.
- **Kill criteria:** (K2) all twist systems infeasible → done, theorem; (K3, corrected) all instances UNSAT → theorem "no srg(333,166,82,83) admits a fixed-point-free Z37 normalized by a complementing map"; extension to "no self-complementary srg(333) with fpf Z37" needs one extra Sylow/Frattini lemma and carries a 37²∤|Aut| caveat. UNKNOWN after ~1 week of solver budget → record the gate + mini-theorems and stop.
- **Publishable even on failure:** the 208× gate narrowing (3/625), the forced cycle-type theorem (π ∈ {(4,4,1),(8,1)}, σ⁴=id splits), and the bonus mini-theorem **no fixed-point-free order-3 automorphism exists, so Z37 is the unique maximal semiregular group** — framed as extending Mathon's self-complementary-SRG program (Discrete Math. 69 (1988) 263–281) to the smallest open conference order, citing Peisert (J. Algebra 240 (2001)) and Klin–Kriger (2016). A SAT hit constructs H(668) outright.

### ACTION 2 — Bank the theorem package (zero compute, P(publishable result) ≈ 1, ~1–2 weeks writing)

Five machine-verified theorems from this round materially upgrade the paper and are unclaimed in the literature:

1. **FPF completeness:** every order-37 automorphism of srg(333,166,82,83) is fixed-point-free (rank/Galois + Perron pincer, sharp at c=9; survives Babai's bound only kills c≥5). Consequence: the 2901-rep/625-class enumeration is exhaustive for **all** order-37 symmetry — the project's FPF hypothesis is upgraded from ansatz to theorem. This also closes the "37 fixed points / srg(37,18,8,9) fixed subgraph" branch flagged elsewhere.
2. **No FPF 3-power automorphism** of any conference graph on a non-square number of vertices; corollary (via Fein–Kantor–Schacher, CFSG-dependent): every vertex-transitive srg(333) admits an fpf Z37 — the existing enumeration covers all vertex-transitive examples. (De Winter–Kamischke–Wang, arXiv:1411.3429, explicitly exclude conference graphs from Benson-type theorems, so the half-case parity gates here are new in print.)
3. **Order-83 structure theorem:** any order-83 automorphism of the 334-point two-graph has 2 fixed points + 4 orbits; the orbit matrix is UNIQUE up to relabeling (diag (40,40,42,42), b12=42, {b13,b14}={37,46}, b34=41; pinned by x²+y²=333); order-41/82 multipliers empty. Independently re-derived and machine-verified twice. Publishable standalone (first nontrivial automorphism constraint ever published for these parameters).
4. **Two-circulant C(334) rigidity package:** D=−A forced (degree-83 real subfield has no quadratic subfield), A-symmetry forced mod 4 in the general DGS form for odd m, row sums pinned (18,3), order-83/166 multipliers dead; plus "no 2-block group-developed H(668) over any group of order 334" (668 not a sum of two squares).
5. **Negative closures worth recording:** the F37 wild-code tower admits a universal solution N = R̄ + (−εεᵀ/18)·u¹⁸(1+u)⁻⁹ for EVERY admissible orbit matrix (so no constraint expressible over F37[Z37] without the ±1-cube condition can eliminate any class — sharpens the collapse theorem to the ramified prime); the π-adic tower is homogeneous below level 36 (zero-tail always feasible); the Pell/theta lattice functor forgets C entirely (lattice ≅ Zⁿ at q=37); Δ₃ SDP = 334 forced; three two-graph cut-and-paste lemmas; central-Z111 dichotomy with the 40/625-class gate.

- **First step:** new sections in `paper/h668_z37.tex` + verification scripts to github.com/maceoCK/h668-z37; items 1–2 are ~20-line Mathlib-ready arguments for `h668lean/`.
- **Kill criteria:** none needed — content is verified.

### ACTION 3 — Corrected SAT+CAS pilot on the open order-2/order-3 multiplier cases (idea I6; refuted as specified, viable as corrected)

This is the only route that targets the project's actual confined open cases with a certificate. The verifier found a **fatal bug in the original spec**: the spectral propagator enforced multiplicity a_t=4 uniformly, but a_t is provably 4 at QR-character embeddings and 5 at QNR embeddings (Galois swap; Σa_t=162) — uniform caps would prune every true completion and produce false certified UNSATs (the exact bug class the project retracted once before). The corrected propagator (per-embedding caps; 6 embeddings for order-3 = 3 QR + 3 QNR; 18 for order-2; 585 vs 855 equations) is the publishable methodological novelty: nobody has put an exact Cauchy-interlacing/Hermitian-completability check inside CDCL, and nobody has aimed proof-logged SAT at any SRG/two-graph parameter set (zero precedent in the literature hunt).

- **Exact first step:** build the minimal IPASIR-UP interlacing propagator (exact integer Sturm over the period lattice, per-embedding a_t) for CaDiCaL 2.0; in parallel dump the same system as OPB into RoundingSat. **Decisive 600s test:** rerun the measured baseline — order-3 skeleton class 1 with the 3 pinned full-diagonal assignments where plain CP-SAT is UNKNOWN at 40s (`/tmp/cc_probe.py` seeds 0–2). Mandatory: corrected propagator must ACCEPT the P(9)/P(25) true positives first (they exhibit the QR/QNR flip and would have caught the bug).
- **Tools/compute:** 2–3 weeks dev reusing `cyclo_cpsat.py` tables; pilot ~$100; full certified sweep honestly re-priced at **$25–40k** (not $5–10k) — only spend if the pilot extrapolates under 2e5 CPU-h.
- **Kill criteria:** pinned leaves unrefuted at 600s in BOTH lanes; cut-fire rate <1% of conflicts; pilot extrapolation >2e5 CPU-h. Failure verdict is itself publishable calibration: "no current proof system + realistic budget certifies the order-2/3 cases at this encoding."
- **Cheap add-ons for the already-running sweeps (bank now, hours of work):** z-flag degree cuts (order-3: degrees ∈{1,4,7}, even count of odd; order-2: all even); and — gated by a <1h non-collapse check (sample (n₊,n₋) over lifts sharing one orbit matrix, or the srg(45)/Z5 analog) — the 3-modular nilpotency-rank machinery (idea I5, not refuted): per-class F₇₂₉ feasibility of the two 9×9 twisted-Hermitian quadratics (~486 bits) could unconditionally kill order-3 classes; corrections to apply: row-sum bound 167<3⁵ justifies mod-3⁵ exactness, use ≤4×4 minors, F_3⁹ for order-2, add Hodge–Newton polygon cuts.

---

## 2. ALL ROUTES CONSIDERED — one-line verdicts

| # | Route | Verdict |
|---|-------|---------|
| A1 | Two-circulant C(334) over Z167 ([[A,B],[Bᵀ,−A]], (s_A,s_B)=(18,3)) | **VIABLE** new cell, never searched at 334 (lit. stops at 86); doubly-symmetric subcell = Turyn-type Williamson(167) = negacyclic C(334) — possibly already dead via Eades 1977 (check thesis first); priors unfavorable (0/3 non-Paley empty, incl. exhaustive general-B emptiness at C(46) verified in-session); metaheuristic only, no certificate — run opportunistically after Actions 1–3 |
| A2 | Order-83 automorphism → unique 4×4 Z83 skeleton | **VIABLE**, structure theorem PROVEN (bank in Action 2); search sizes corrected (flavor (ii) is 2^354.5 not 2^271 — S12, S34 are free; flavor (i) at 2^323 is the rigid one); full closure likely beyond solvers |
| A3 | Order-37 automorphism WITH fixed points | **OBSTRUCTED** — theorem, all 8 mixed classes dead; FPF is WLOG (Action 2) |
| A4 | Order-3 / order-9 semiregular (3-power FPF) | **OBSTRUCTED** — theorem; vertex-transitive ⇒ fpf Z37 corollary (Action 2) |
| A5 | Non-cyclic groups (Z37:Z3, Z37:Z9, Z3×Z3, Z3×F, Z3²×Z37, Z333) | **OBSTRUCTED** in all assigned forms (Theorems A–C, verified); survivors: known open order-2/3 multiplier cases + NEW central-Z111 slice (f=111, type 1³3², 40/625 classes, ~608 bits) = **VIABLE** secondary CP-SAT target |
| A6 | Williamson/GS/2-block ansätze over Z167 with multipliers | **OBSTRUCTED** — proven empty (constant PAFs; 668 ≠ sum of two squares); unique survivor is A1 |
| A7 | Two-graph expansion/composition/switching to 334 | **OBSTRUCTED** — all families provably miss 334; three new pre-kill lemmas (bank in Action 2) |
| A8 | k-point SDP / equiangular lines (167, 1/√333) | **OBSTRUCTED** — Δ₃ statistics fully determined, dLMV Δ₆=Δ₃ collapse regime, no SDP has ever killed LP-tight parameters; skip even the due-diligence run |
| I1 | π-adic Stickelberger tower at (1−ζ37) | **REFUTED** — system homogeneous below level 36, zero-tail universally feasible; salvage: trivial level-1 cuts + a possible level-36 (inhomogeneous) restart as a NEW costed item |
| I2 | Lattices / theta / Pell Grams | **CLOSED** — lattice ≅ Zⁿ at the model prime; ≥127× coefficient deficit; supports prioritizing the lines/Gram formulation only |
| I3 | F37 wild-prime self-dual code tower | **REFUTED** — explicit universal solution for every orbit matrix, ≥180-dim variety; salvage: [334,167]₃₇ self-dual lemma + the "no F37[Z37]-level obstruction exists" negative theorem (Action 2) |
| I4 | Annealed counting / symmetry-tilted E_d | **PARTIALLY REFUTED** — E_d model wrong (E₉(333)=2^−27.4, not 2^+39; both multiplier layers annealed-dead); survives: threshold theorem E_pairs(333)=2^−1.37 at l≈330, calibration flat to l=21, retire-unstructured-search conclusion; optional cheap 90-bit d=9 CP-SAT run = near-certain structured negative |
| I5 | 3-modular nilpotency-rank pair (n₊,n₋) | **VIABLE (gated)** — not refuted twice; genuinely non-trace-linear; run the <1h non-collapse gate, then per-class F₇₂₉ feasibility (could close order-3 classes unconditionally) |
| I6 | Two-lane proof-logged SAT+CAS | **VIABLE after correction** (Action 3); original spec REFUTED (uniform a_t=4 = false-negative generator) |
| I7 | Finite-geometry embeddings/modifications | **CLOSED** (integrality of geometric SRG eigenvalues); residue = A1 |
| I8 | Fourier/uncertainty/flatness/field-descent on pairs | **CLOSED** — UP sits at the realizable equality threshold; sum-of-two-norms breaks all descent; one optional 1h Pari/GP ideal-level re-test before burial |
| I9 | CRT quotient gluing 9×37 | **CLOSED** — 37-side has ~10^24.6 compressed classes; 108/166 constraint classes invisible to every quotient; reduces to existing lead #1 |
| I10 | Self-complementary Z37⋊Z4 | **VIABLE — TOP PICK** (Action 1, with the 415-bit encoding correction) |
| L1 | Noncyclic (334,2,333,166)-RDS in groups of order 668 (Z2²×Z167, D334, Dic167) | **UNCLEAR/unexamined** — evades the order-3-character theorem; Arasu–Chen–Pott broke the prime-power barrier once; cheap character-feasibility check is a candidate next-round item |
| L2 | Bordered two-circulant-core C(334) over Z166 | **UNCLEAR** — literature blank; arithmetic feasibility never written down |
| L3 | Star complements + SAT | **UNCLEAR** — only technique with a confirmed two-graph kill (RT(96)), but leverage weak at multiplicities 166/166; never tried, never even published as a negative |
| L4 | Invariant SDP / Terwilliger 3-point blocks for Z37:Z3; matrix field descent det(M_t)det(M_t+I)=83⁹; Kajiura–Matsumoto quotient coupling | **UNCLEAR/untried** — the three surviving beyond-trace-linear families; each has a finite entry point; none costed this round |
| L5 | ML/LLM-guided search | **NO direct transfer** — every verified success is below-frontier, tiny, or soft-objective; salvage: AlphaMapleSAT cubing (1.6–7.6×) inside Action 3, PatternBoost symmetry-mining at solvable conference orders as a cheap idea-generator |

---

## 3. Literature findings that change project assumptions

1. **The negacyclic C(334) question is a citable loose end at exactly our order.** Published verification of the DGS conjecture reaches only v ≤ 226 (Đoković–Kotsireas, arXiv:1508.00640), while Seberry attributes a v < 1000 determination to Eades' 1977 ANU thesis (http://groupexcelindia.com/stjosephs/pdf/naga-cyclic.pdf). Via the Kovacs–Eades lemma, negacyclic C(334) ⟺ the doubly-symmetric two-circulant cell of A1. **Obtain Eades' thesis before spending any compute on that cell** — it may be closable by citation; the general-B cell is the genuinely open one.
2. **Sound gauge-fixing now has a published foundation**: Bulutoglu–Baczkowski–Yauney (arXiv:2604.22423, Apr 2026) classify the full LP equivalence group G₁⋊(G₂⋊G₃); and VeriPB certified dominance/symmetry breaking (Bogaerts–Gocht–McCreesh–Nordström, JAIR 77 (2023), arXiv:2203.12275) + IPASIR-UP (SAT 2023) make WLOG reasoning machine-checkable — a direct, sound replacement for the project's retracted base-point gauge trick. (Caveat: dynamic SMS-style breaking is currently uncertifiable; use static lex-leader clauses.)
3. **No scoop risk, no template**: (333,166,82,83)/C(334) appears in 2015–2026 literature only as '?' in Brouwer's and Balonin–Seberry's tables; LP(333) is named only aspirationally (ISSAC 2025, DOI 10.1145/3747199.3747549); and **no published work has ever combined orbit matrices with SAT/certificates on any SRG/two-graph/conference parameter set** — the project is composing two literatures never joined (opportunity and warning both).
4. **Conflicting reports on C(66)** — one agent claims Gritsenko (arXiv:2102.05432) constructed srg(65,32,15,16), settling C(66) positively and making C(86) the smallest open conference order; two other hunts (and Wikipedia/OEIS A000952) report 66 still open. **Resolve before citing anywhere.** If true, it strengthens the "conference parameters tend to exist" prior and weakens the Mathon-only-escape narrative.
5. **New non-trace-linear kill technology exists**: Kajiura–Matsumoto (arXiv:2512.24597, Dec 2025) closed the 70-year-open (120,35,10) difference set by coupling quotient equi-distribution data with quadratic ILP constraints — structurally matched to coupling the 9- and 37-quotients of 333; untested beyond its flagship case.
6. **Conference graphs are explicitly excluded** from the standard Benson-type automorphism theorems (De Winter–Kamischke–Wang, arXiv:1411.3429) and from the critical-group nonexistence machinery (Ducey et al., arXiv:1910.07686) — so the parity/fixicity gates proven this round are publishable, not folklore.
7. **Internal but assumption-changing**: (a) FPF for order 37 is now a theorem, making the 2901-rep enumeration complete for all order-37 (and all vertex-transitive) symmetry; (b) l=333 sits at the annealed existence threshold (E_pairs = 2^−1.37, crossing ≈330; mod-4 PAF lattice worth 165 bits over the naive model) — unstructured PT/SA is hunting a ~2^−29-per-class event and should be retired; if LP(333) exists it is algebraic.

---

## 4. Honest overall assessment

**Does anything here materially raise the chance of settling H(668)?** Modestly, and asymmetrically. On the construction side, the round produced exactly two genuinely new, never-probed search cells with real (if small) hit probability: the self-complementary Z37⋊Z4 ansatz (3 skeleton classes, 415-bit instances — the best-gated constructive bet the project has ever had) and the two-circulant Z167 cell (priors poor: 0/3 non-Paley orders empty, all Turyn-type solutions ≤99 Paley-explained). On the nonexistence side, the round was brutal and informative: **four independent obstruction lenses (π-adic tower, F37 code tower, theta/lattices, k-point SDP) were each shown to admit universal/forced feasibility** — every relaxation that forgets ±1-integrality is provably blind, extending the trace-linearity collapse to the ramified prime and to the continuous setting. Any kill must engage the cube nonlinearly inside a proof system (Action 3) or via the two unrefuted gated invariants (I5; L4 family). This pattern is weak Bayesian evidence that the object exists.

**Realistically reachable this year:** (i) the theorem package (Action 2) — near-certain, and it converts the paper into the definitive automorphism-method survey of srg(333,166,82,83): FPF completeness, no 3-power FPF + vertex-transitive corollary, the unique order-83 skeleton, the two-circulant rigidity package, and the universal-solution negatives; (ii) certified closure of one or two symmetry branches — the self-complementary branch (Action 1) is the most likely full closure; the order-3 multiplier case via corrected SAT+CAS (Action 3) is plausible if the 600s pilot fires, and would be the **first machine-certified nonexistence result for any SRG/two-graph parameter set** — publishable regardless of which way it goes; (iii) settling H(668) itself: probability low (I'd put the combined constructive channels at a few percent this year). The honest headline: this round did not find the door, but it proved where the door cannot be — and left three specific, cheap, falsifiable places to push.
